ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for Jemput-Jemput Pisang; mapped to the closest parent class 'fritter (food product)' as a defensible approximate anchor.
ontology.foodex2_description: Banana fritter — deep-fried battered banana snack; mapped under FoodEx2 group 'Fried snacks and savoury snacks' as closest defensible code.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No dedicated FoodEx2 code for Jemput-Jemput Pisang. Code A0FJP used as closest category anchor for fried snack products containing fruit. Reviewer should confirm or replace with authoritative FoodEx2 lookup.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical concept applicable to this traditional snack food.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Jemput-Jemput Pisang is 3 to 4 small ball-shaped fritters, weighing approximately 100 g in total, commonly served as a tea-time snack.

nutrient_source.title: Energy and Nutrient Composition of Foods — Singapore HPB Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Jemput-Jemput Pisang in the HPB Singapore food composition database. Values adapted from HPB entries for deep-fried banana fritters and fried Malay snacks, cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central entries for banana fritters (USDA SR Legacy). Input JSON values from the enriched pipeline were retained where consistent with these approved sources. Saturated fat estimated from typical palm oil deep-frying profile per HPB fried snack data.

gi_source.title: International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values — Banana-based fried snack analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Jemput-Jemput Pisang in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 65 estimated by analogy with deep-fried banana fritters and refined-flour fried snacks containing ripe banana and added sugar, as documented in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008) International Tables of GI and GL Values. The fat content from deep-frying moderates the glycemic response relative to plain banana. Glycemic load per 100 g serving calculated as (65 × 42.5) / 100 = 27.6.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (85 mg per 100 g); high potassium (240 mg) supports blood pressure regulation
hypertension.guidance: Jemput-Jemput Pisang is relatively low in sodium and provides potassium from banana, making it acceptable in moderation for individuals with hypertension, provided deep-fried oil intake is limited overall.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (18.5 g per 100 g), refined wheat flour, and medium GI of 65 with a high glycemic load of 27.6 per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit or avoid Jemput-Jemput Pisang due to its high sugar content, refined carbohydrate base, and medium-to-high glycemic load, which can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (4.8 g per 100 g) and total fat (12.5 g per 100 g) from deep-frying in palm oil; high sugar content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume Jemput-Jemput Pisang only occasionally, as the saturated fat from deep-frying and high sugar content may adversely affect lipid profiles and cardiovascular risk when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Jemput-Jemput Pisang, also known as Cekodok Pisang, is a traditional Malay deep-fried banana fritter popular across Malaysia, Singapore, and Brunei as a tea-time snack. The fritters are made from a simple batter of mashed overripe bananas, wheat flour, sugar, and a pinch of salt, which is dropped by small pinches into hot oil — the name 'jemput' literally means 'to pick up with the fingertips,' describing this traditional shaping method. The result is a small, roughly ball-shaped fritter with a dark golden-brown, slightly crisp exterior and a soft, dense, intensely sweet banana-flavoured interior. Nutritionally, a 100 g serving provides approximately 285 kcal, 3.2 g protein, 12.5 g fat (including approximately 4.8 g saturated fat from palm oil), and 42.5 g carbohydrates with 18.5 g sugar. The food has a medium glycemic index of approximately 65 and a high glycemic load of 27.6 per serving, making it unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or blood sugar levels. It is low in sodium (85 mg) and provides useful amounts of potassium (240 mg) from the banana content. The dish is naturally vegetarian, vegan, halal, and dairy-free, but contains gluten from wheat flour. It is best consumed as an occasional snack rather than a dietary staple due to its high sugar and fat content from deep-frying.
common_user_queries: How many calories in banana fritters?
What are the ingredients in Jemput-Jemput Pisang?
What is the GI of banana fritters?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Jemput-Jemput Pisang was found in the HPB Singapore or IMR Malaysia MyFCD databases. Nutrient values were adapted from HPB fried Malay snack entries and USDA FoodData Central banana fritter analogues. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database. Saturated fat and fatty acid breakdown are estimated from typical palm oil deep-frying profiles. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. water_g set to -1: moisture content for this traditional deep-fried preparation is not reported in HPB Singapore or USDA FoodData Central for an exact Jemput-Jemput Pisang entry. 2. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 and vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0: these are confirmed zero / naturally zero for a plant-based deep-fried snack with no animal-derived ingredients; confirmed consistent with USDA FoodData Central banana fritter data. 3. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: naturally zero as this is a vegan product containing no animal fats or eggs. 4. Saturated fat (4.8 g), monounsaturated fat (5.2 g), polyunsaturated fat (1.8 g), and trans fat (0.1 g) are estimated from typical palm oil deep-frying fatty acid profiles per HPB fried snack data; these values should be confirmed against a laboratory analysis of the specific preparation. 5. FoodEx2 code A0FJP used as closest approximate anchor; reviewer should verify against the authoritative FoodEx2 browser. 6. FoodOn URI mapped to 'fritter (food product)' as the closest parent class; no species-specific or dish-specific FoodOn term was identified. 7. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate; direct measurement of Jemput-Jemput Pisang GI has not been published in the University of Sydney GI Database as of the access date.
